nodes.description: Clavicular abnormalities are a frequent skeletal finding in JATD, part of the generalized ciliopathy-related chondrodysplasia affecting the thoracic skeleton.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000889 | Abnormality of the clavicle | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Metaphyseal changes (irregular, widened or spurred metaphyses) are a frequent radiographic feature of JATD, reflecting the impaired endochondral ossification in ciliopathy-driven chondrodysplasia.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000944 | Abnormal metaphysis morphology | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology, including the trident acetabular roof (acetabular spurs), is a very frequent and pathognomonic radiographic finding in Jeune syndrome.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002644 | Abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: DYNC2H1 mutations cause asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy and short rib-polydactyly syndrome, type III.
evidence.snippet: characterized by short ribs and a narrow thorax, short long bones, inconstant polydactyly, and trident acetabular roof.

nodes.description: Abnormal rib morphology (short, horizontal ribs) is a very frequent skeletal finding in JATD and is the primary contributor to the narrow thorax and respiratory insufficiency.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000772 | Abnormal rib morphology | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Shortening of the digits (brachydactyly) is a frequent skeletal feature of JATD, reflecting the generalized chondrodysplasia affecting phalangeal growth.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001156 | Brachydactyly | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Cone-shaped epiphyses, particularly of the phalanges, are a frequent radiographic finding in JATD, arising from the Hedgehog signaling defect in chondrocytes at the growth plates.

evidence.snippet: HP:0010579 | Cone-shaped epiphysis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Mutations in DYNC2H1 (most common cause) and other dynein-2 complex genes (DYNC2LI1, WDR34, WDR60) disrupt retrograde intraflagellar transport, which is essential for cilium function and Hedgehog signal transduction in developing cartilage and other tissues.

evidence.reference_title: DYNC2H1 mutations cause asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy and short rib-polydactyly syndrome, type III.
evidence.snippet: DYNC2H1 is a component of a cytoplasmic dynein complex and is directly involved in the generation and maintenance of cilia.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies DYNC2H1 mutations as a common cause of asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy (Jeune syndrome) without major polydactyly, renal or retinal involvement.
evidence.snippet: Assessment of fibroblasts from patients showed accumulation of anterograde IFT proteins in the ciliary tips, confirming defects similar to patients with other retrograde IFT machinery mutations

nodes.description: Hepatic fibrosis and biliary dysgenesis, part of the ciliopathy spectrum affecting hepatobiliary development. Reported in 22% of postnatal cases.

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: Renal and liver diseases occurred respectively in 17% and 22% of cases

nodes.description: Mutations in IFT genes (IFT80, IFT140, IFT172, WDR19, and others) disrupt the bidirectional transport system within primary cilia. This impairs ciliary assembly, maintenance, and signaling, particularly Hedgehog pathway transduction required for skeletal development.

evidence.reference_title: IFT80, which encodes a conserved intraflagellar transport protein, is mutated in Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy.
evidence.snippet: IFT80 mutations underlie a subset of Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy cases, establishing the first association of a defective intraflagellar transport (IFT) protein with human disease.

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: To date, mutations in IFT80, DYNC2H1, TTC21B and WDR19 have been reported in ATD. The clinical and molecular heterogeneity leads to difficulties in the evaluation of the long-term prognosis.

nodes.description: Rhizomelic or mesomelic limb shortening, variable in severity.

evidence.reference_title: DYNC2H1 mutations cause asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy and short rib-polydactyly syndrome, type III.
evidence.snippet: characterized by short ribs and a narrow thorax, short long bones, inconstant polydactyly, and trident acetabular roof.

nodes.description: Shortening of the long bones of the limbs (micromelia) is a very frequent feature of JATD, reflecting the ciliopathy-related chondrodysplasia affecting appendicular skeletal growth.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002983 | Micromelia | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: DYNC2H1 mutations cause asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy and short rib-polydactyly syndrome, type III.
evidence.snippet: characterized by short ribs and a narrow thorax, short long bones, inconstant polydactyly, and trident acetabular roof.

nodes.description: Characteristic narrow, bell-shaped thorax with short horizontal ribs. The cardinal feature causing respiratory insufficiency, which is the primary cause of mortality in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: IFT80, which encodes a conserved intraflagellar transport protein, is mutated in Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy.
evidence.snippet: often leads to death in infancy because of a severely constricted thoracic cage and respiratory insufficiency

evidence.reference_title: DYNC2H1 mutations cause asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy and short rib-polydactyly syndrome, type III.
evidence.snippet: Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy (ATD) is an autosomal-recessive chondrodysplasia characterized by short ribs and a narrow thorax, short long bones, inconstant polydactyly, and trident acetabular roof.

nodes.description: Postaxial polydactyly of hands and/or feet, present in a subset of patients. DYNC2H1 patients tend to lack polydactyly.

evidence.reference_title: IFT80, which encodes a conserved intraflagellar transport protein, is mutated in Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy.
evidence.snippet: retinal degeneration, cystic renal disease and polydactyly may be complicating features.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies DYNC2H1 mutations as a common cause of asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy (Jeune syndrome) without major polydactyly, renal or retinal involvement.
evidence.snippet: DYNC2H1 mutations as a common cause of asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy (Jeune syndrome) without major polydactyly, renal or retinal involvement.

nodes.description: Respiratory insufficiency from thoracic restriction is the primary cause of mortality. Noted in 60% of postnatal cases, with some requiring tracheostomy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002093 | Respiratory insufficiency | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: pulmonary insufficiency was noted in 60% of cases, with tracheotomy requirement in five cases.

nodes.description: Progressive renal cystic disease that may lead to renal failure. Occurs in a significant proportion of patients who survive infancy, reported in 17% of postnatal cases.

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: Renal and liver diseases occurred respectively in 17% and 22% of cases

nodes.description: Primary cilium dysfunction leads to renal cystic disease through disrupted planar cell polarity and flow-sensing mechanisms in renal tubular epithelium, similar to other ciliopathies such as autosomal recessive polycystic kidney disease.

evidence.reference_title: IFT80, which encodes a conserved intraflagellar transport protein, is mutated in Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Knockdown of ift80 in zebrafish resulted in cystic kidneys

nodes.description: Retinal degeneration may occur as part of the ciliopathy phenotype. Present in 50% of cases older than 5 years, more frequent than previously appreciated.

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: retinal alteration was present in 50% of cases aged more than 5 years.

evidence.reference_title: IFT80, which encodes a conserved intraflagellar transport protein, is mutated in Jeune asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy.
evidence.snippet: retinal degeneration, cystic renal disease and polydactyly may be complicating features.

nodes.description: Horizontally oriented short ribs contributing to the constricted thoracic cage and respiratory compromise.

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: Asphyxiating Thoracic Dysplasia (ATD) belongs to the short rib polydactyly group and is characterized by a narrow thorax, short long bones and trident acetabular roof.

nodes.description: A short thorax is a very frequent radiological hallmark of Jeune syndrome, contributing to the defining narrow bell-shaped thoracic cage that causes respiratory insufficiency.

evidence.snippet: HP:0010306 | Short thorax | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Asphyxiating thoracic dysplasia: clinical and molecular review of 39 families.
evidence.snippet: Asphyxiating Thoracic Dysplasia (ATD) belongs to the short rib polydactyly group and is characterized by a narrow thorax, short long bones and trident acetabular roof.

nodes.description: Generalized skeletal dysplasia is the overarching phenotype of JATD, encompassing the short ribs, limb shortening, and pelvic/metaphyseal changes due to ciliary IFT dysfunction affecting chondrocyte signaling.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002652 | Skeletal dysplasia | Very frequent (99-80%)
